evals.prompt: Here's a new client brief. Read the file at .claude/skills/client-content-creator/evals/files/fitness-brand-brief.md and create a full content package for Apex Strength. No topic preference — ideate based on the business.
evals.expected_output: A complete content package with all 5 pieces: (1) a 500-700 word SEO blog post about strength training or fitness coaching with a strong hook and embedded CTA, (2) an Instagram caption ~150-180 words with 10-12 hashtags, (3) a LinkedIn post adapted for a professional audience, (4) an 8-slide Instagram carousel with headings, sub-text, and visual concepts, (5) a 6-8 scene video/reel concept with timing and audio direction. The writing must reflect Apex's direct, no-nonsense voice — no motivational poster language, no 'transform your body' phrasing, no em dashes.
expectations: The blog post is between 500 and 700 words
The blog post opens with a specific scene, moment, or claim — not a definition or backstory paragraph
The blog post contains zero em dashes (—)
The Instagram caption is between 130 and 200 words (not counting hashtags)
The Instagram caption includes at least 8 hashtags
A LinkedIn post is present and distinct from the Instagram caption
The carousel section includes exactly 8 slides with headings and sub-text
The reel concept includes at least 6 scenes with time codes
The output does not use the phrase 'transform your body' or 'melt fat'
The writing reflects Apex's direct, practical voice — not generic motivational language

evals.prompt: I just onboarded a new client, Copper & Ground — a specialty coffee shop in Brooklyn. Their brand brief is at .claude/skills/client-content-creator/evals/files/coffee-brand-brief.md. Generate their full content package. For the blog, focus on the topic: why light-roast coffee tastes completely different from what most people expect.
evals.expected_output: A complete content package with all 5 pieces adapted to Copper & Ground's warm, curious, non-snobby voice. Blog post covers light roast expectations vs reality. Instagram and LinkedIn are distinct adaptations. Carousel and reel reflect the brand's copper/cream aesthetic. Zero em dashes throughout. No generic coffee marketing language ('artisanal', 'best in Brooklyn', unexplained jargon).
expectations: The blog post topic is specifically about light-roast coffee expectations/reality
The blog post is between 500 and 700 words
The blog post contains zero em dashes (—)
The Instagram caption uses a hook line in the first sentence (not context, not backstory)
The Instagram caption includes hashtags
The reel concept references the brand's copper or cream color palette
The carousel includes exactly 8 slides
The content does not use the word 'artisanal'
The LinkedIn post takes a thought-leadership angle, not just a copy of the Instagram post
Brand alignment notes section is present at the end

evals.prompt:
Client just sent me their info inline — no file. Here it is:

Business: Verdant Architecture Studio, NYC
What we do: Sustainable residential architecture and interior design. We design homes that are energy-efficient, biophilic (lots of natural materials, plants, light), and built to last 50+ years. Projects are mostly in the $800K-$3M range.
Audience: Wealthy homeowners in the tri-state area, 38-60 years old, environmentally conscious, appreciate craft and quality over trend.
Voice: Thoughtful, considered, unhurried. We don't chase trends. We believe good design is invisible — it just feels right. Slightly academic but never exclusionary.
Colors: Forest green #2D4A3E, Warm sand #C4A882, Off-white #F2EDE6, Charcoal #3A3A3A
Fonts: headings in Freight Display, body in Gill Sans
Create a content package, ideate the topics.
evals.expected_output: A complete 5-piece content package for Verdant Architecture Studio, written in a thoughtful, unhurried, slightly academic tone. Topics should relate to sustainable design, biophilic architecture, or the philosophy of building for longevity. Blog is 500-700 words. All pieces are distinct and platform-native. Zero em dashes. No trend-chasing or generic 'modern design' language.
expectations: The skill correctly extracted business info from inline text (no files provided)
The blog post is between 500 and 700 words
The blog post contains zero em dashes (—)
The blog or social post topics relate to sustainable design, biophilic architecture, or building longevity
The voice is thoughtful and considered — not punchy or trend-focused
The carousel includes exactly 8 slides with visual concepts referencing forest green or warm sand tones
The reel concept is present with at least 6 scenes
The LinkedIn post is distinct from the Instagram caption
No generic design phrases like 'breathtaking', 'stunning', or 'transform your space'
